leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer  Producer note: reworked per rework note — eliminated the second source of truth for pool amounts using the note's primary form: `EpochConfig.poolRflr` is now a `bigint` referencing the canonical pool constants (`DEC25_REWARD_POOL`/`JAN26_REWARD_POOL`/`FEB26_REWARD_POOL`/`MAR26_REWARD_POOL`), and `RewardsInfo` formats it at render time via `formatUnits` + `Intl.NumberFormat`. Change a pool constant and /rewards now follows automatically. Date strings remain as-is in `REWARD_EPOCHS` (sole source, per the note). Render tests added asserting the derived values appear.  ## QA evidence  **Tests added** (`src/lib/components/RewardsInfo.test.ts`; `data-testid="reward-epochs"` added to the epoch list for targeting):  - `sources each epoch pool from the canonical reward pool constants` — identity check: `REWARD_EPOCHS[i].poolRflr` equals the four canonical bigint constants in order - `renders one entry per epoch with the pool amount derived from its bigint constant` — renders the component and asserts each `<li>` shows the label, dates, and `formatted(poolRflr) rFLR` - `renders the exact formatted pool strings for the current constants` — pins today's exact strings: `1,000,000 rFLR`, `500,000 rFLR` twice, `300,000 rFLR`  **Mutation proof — fails on the hand-written-strings behavior the fix removed.**  Mutation 1: reverted `poolRflr` to the PR's previous hand-written display strings (`"1,000,000"`, ...) and the component to printing them raw. 2/4 tests fail:  ``` FAIL src/lib/components/RewardsInfo.test.ts > RewardsInfo Component > sources each epoch pool from the canonical reward pool constants AssertionError: expected [ '1,000,000', '500,000', …(2) ] to deeply equal [ 1000000000000000000000000n, …(3) ] FAIL src/lib/components/RewardsInfo.test.ts > RewardsInfo Component > renders one entry per epoch with the pool amount derived from its bigint constant Tests  2 failed | 2 passed (4) ```  (The exact-strings test alone passes under this mutation — hand-written strings agree with the constants *today* — which is exactly why the derivation + identity tests are the enforcers, not just string pins.)  Mutation 2: simulated the silent-lie failure mode — kept the fix but hand-wrote a stale `"900,000"` display for the December epoch. 2/4 tests fail:  ``` FAIL src/lib/components/RewardsInfo.test.ts > RewardsInfo Component > renders one entry per epoch with the pool amount derived from its bigint constant AssertionError: expected 'December 2025: December 1, 2025 00:00…' to contain '1,000,000 rFLR' FAIL src/lib/components/RewardsInfo.test.ts > RewardsInfo Component > renders the exact formatted pool strings for the current constants AssertionError: expected 'December 2025: December 1, 2025 00:00…' to contain '1,000,000 rFLR' Tests  2 failed | 2 passed (4) ```  Fix restored after each mutation.  **Scoped suite result:** 4/4 passed (`RewardsInfo.test.ts` is the only consumer of `REWARD_EPOCHS`/`EpochConfig`):  ```  Test Files  1 passed (1)       Tests  4 passed (4) ```  Prettier/pre-commit pass on the changed files. Branch merged with `origin/main` (clean merge) before the rework.
leaks.title: fix: drive RewardsInfo from REWARD_EPOCHS constant, remove hardcoded Jan-2025 text (#340)

this also brings the branch past the #394-adjacent changes on `main`).  **What changed** (commit 8b9e549)  - `src/lib/components/ReceiptsTable.test.ts`:   - `re-renders rows when the receipts prop is updated (#360)` — mounts ReceiptsTable, updates the `receipts` prop via `rerender`, asserts row values and row count update.   - `renders the "0.00000" fallback for malformed balances instead of throwing (#358)` — rows whose `balance` arrives as `undefined` / `""` render the `0.00000` fallback in the held/total cells instead of throwing. - `src/lib/components/ReceiptsTable.svelte`: restored the `// Guard against undefined values`, `// Calculate totals: (balance * 10^18) / tokenId ...`, and `// Calculate per-receipt: 10^36 / tokenId ...` comments; no behavior change.  **QA evidence**  Mutation validation 1 — reverted `$: mappedReceipts` to `const mappedReceipts` (the #360 bug): the new rerender test fails, rest pass:  ``` FAIL  src/lib/components/ReceiptsTable.test.ts > ReceiptsTable Component > re-renders rows when the receipts prop is updated (#360) Error: expect(element).toHaveTextContent() Expected element to have text content:   2.50000 Received:   1.00000  ❯ src/lib/components/ReceiptsTable.test.ts:90:49  Tests  1 failed | 3 passed (4) ```  Mutation validation 2 — reverted the held-cell template to direct `formatUnits(receipt.balance, token.decimals)` (the #358 bug): the new fallback test fails with the exact production failure mode, rest pass:  ``` FAIL  src/lib/components/ReceiptsTable.test.ts > ReceiptsTable Component > renders the "0.00000" fallback for malformed balances instead of throwing (#358) TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString')  ❯ Module.formatUnits node_modules/viem/utils/unit/formatUnits.ts:17:23  ❯ Array.create_default_slot_8 src/lib/components/ReceiptsTable.svelte:85:21  Tests  1 failed | 3 passed (4) ```  Both mutants restored; suite results on the fixed code:  - Scoped: `npx vitest run src/lib/components/ReceiptsTable.test.ts` — 4/4 pass. - Full: `npx vitest run` — 53 files, 285/285 pass.

leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer  Producer note: reworked per rework note — pinned both previously-untested behaviors of the shared `Input.svelte` with discriminating tests: (1) programmatic `bind:amount` updates (initial and post-mount) now asserted to route through `handleDecimalSeparator`, via a new `InputTest.svelte` bind harness; (2) the default validator now asserted to reject both `""` and a lone `"."` (surfacing "Invalid amount") while leaving the bound `amount` untouched. To make the `""` rejection hold, the default validator was tightened to also reject the empty string (it previously only rejected `"."` and NaN), matching the org empty-fraction ruling and the #330 proposed fix.  ## QA evidence  **Tests added** (`src/lib/components/Input.test.ts`, plus `src/lib/components/InputTest.svelte` bind:amount harness):  - `external amount updates > sanitizes the initial amount through handleDecimalSeparator` - `external amount updates > routes programmatic bind:amount updates through handleDecimalSeparator` - `default validator > rejects a lone '.' set via bind:amount and leaves amount untouched` - `default validator > rejects an empty string set via bind:amount and leaves amount untouched`  **Mutation proof — fails on pre-fix behavior.**  Mutation A: reverted the external-update fix (raw `displayValue = amount.toString()` initializer + old reactive sync block from main), kept the validator. 4/4 new tests fail:  ``` FAIL src/lib/components/Input.test.ts > Input > external amount updates > sanitizes the initial amount through handleDecimalSeparator AssertionError: expected '1,5' to be '1.5' // Object.is equality FAIL src/lib/components/Input.test.ts > Input > external amount updates > routes programmatic bind:amount updates through handleDecimalSeparator AssertionError: expected '1,5e10abc' to be '1.510' // Object.is equality FAIL src/lib/components/Input.test.ts > Input > default validator > rejects a lone '.' set via bind:amount and leaves amount untouched FAIL src/lib/components/Input.test.ts > Input > default validator > rejects an empty string set via bind:amount and leaves amount untouched Tests  4 failed | 7 passed (11) ```  Mutation B: reverted the default validator to main's no-op `() => undefined`, kept the external-update fix. Both default-validator tests fail:  ``` FAIL src/lib/components/Input.test.ts > Input > default validator > rejects a lone '.' set via bind:amount and leaves amount untouched TestingLibraryElementError: Unable to find an element with the text: Invalid amount. FAIL src/lib/components/Input.test.ts > Input > default validator > rejects an empty string set via bind:amount and leaves amount untouched TestingLibraryElementError: Unable to find an element with the text: Invalid amount. Tests  2 failed | 9 passed (11) ```  Fix restored after each mutation; suite green again.  **Scoped suite result:** 62/62 passed across the component and its consumers — `Input.test.ts` (11), `handleDecimalSeparator.test.ts`, `Lock.test.ts` (15), `DsfStrategy.test.ts`, `DcaStrategy.test.ts`:  ```  Test Files  5 passed (5)       Tests  62 passed (62) ```  Prettier/pre-commit pass on the changed files. Branch merged with `origin/main` (clean merge) before the rework.
leaks.title: fix: Input.svelte sanitize external updates and tighten default validator (#328, #330)

leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer  Producer note: reworked per rework note — added three discriminating tests to `DsfStrategy.test.ts` pinning each gate fix (#332 multiplier/timePerEpoch validation, #335 rotateDepositAmount gating, #336 the previously-dead overrideDepositAmount guard). Each fails on the pre-fix code; the pre-existing 14-test suite passes on both sides, confirming the gates were unpinned before. Branch merged up to current `main`. Tests only; no component changes.  ## QA evidence  **Tests added** (all in `src/lib/components/DsfStrategy.test.ts`)  1. Issue #332 — `it.each` over the three period inputs × two invalid values (6 cases): `should show a validation error and disable Deploy when <next-trade-multiplier-input|cost-basis-multiplier-input|time-per-epoch-input> is set to <"0"|"">`. Asserts Deploy enabled before, then the rendered validation error (`Period must be greater than 0` / `Period is required`, the observable of the bound error flag) AND Deploy disabled after. (The Input component's decimal sanitizer strips `-`, so `"0"` and `""` are the invalid states reachable through the input.) 2. Issue #335 — `should disable Deploy when the rotate deposit amount is empty or zero`: custom deposit on, amount-token deposit filled, rotate deposit empty → disabled; rotate `"0"` → error text + disabled; rotate `"25"` → enabled (proves the gate is the rotate value). 3. Issue #336 — `should disable Deploy when the override deposit amount is zero`: custom deposit on, rotate deposit valid, override deposit `"0"` (the `0n` state where the old `overrideDepositAmount == undefined` check could never fire) → error text + disabled; `"50"` → enabled.  **Fails-on-base proof** — with `src/lib/components/DsfStrategy.svelte` and `src/lib/trade/validateDeploymentArgs.ts` reverted to the `origin/main` (pre-fix) versions and the new tests in place:  ``` Tests  8 failed | 14 passed (22) ❯ should show a validation error and disable Deploy when next-trade-multiplier-input is set to "0"   → Unable to find an element with the text: Period must be greater than 0. ❯ should show a validation error and disable Deploy when next-trade-multiplier-input is set to ""   → Unable to find an element with the text: Period is required. ❯ should show a validation error and disable Deploy when cost-basis-multiplier-input is set to "0"   → Unable to find an element with the text: Period must be greater than 0. ❯ should show a validation error and disable Deploy when cost-basis-multiplier-input is set to ""   → Unable to find an element with the text: Period is required. ❯ should show a validation error and disable Deploy when time-per-epoch-input is set to "0"   → Unable to find an element with the text: Period must be greater than 0. ❯ should show a validation error and disable Deploy when time-per-epoch-input is set to ""   → Unable to find an element with the text: Period is required. ❯ should disable Deploy when the rotate deposit amount is empty or zero   → expect(element).toBeDisabled()        // Deploy was ENABLED with rotate deposit empty ❯ should disable Deploy when the override deposit amount is zero   → Unable to find an element with the text: Override deposit amount must be greater than 0. ```  All 8 new tests fail on pre-fix code — one per gate change, including the `toBeDisabled()` failure showing Deploy stayed clickable with an empty rotate deposit — and all 14 pre-existing tests still pass on pre-fix code (the suite previously could not see these gates). With the fix restored: 22/22 pass.  **Oracle:** expected behavior derives from the deploy-gate contract (Deploy must be disabled while any deployment argument is absent or non-positive) and the validator messages in `src/lib/trade/validateDeploymentArgs.ts`, not from the `disableDeploy` expression under test.  **Category check:** rework note asks three discriminating tests, one per issue (#332, #335, #336), each failing on pre-fix code; covered — #332 across all three period inputs and both invalid-value kinds, #335 across both empty and zero rotate deposits plus the enabling counter-case, #336 at the exact previously-unreachable `0n` state plus the enabling counter-case.  **Scoped suite:** `npm test -- run src/lib/components/DsfStrategy.test.ts` — 22/22 pass (14 pre-existing + 8 new) on the fixed code; prettier and eslint clean.
leaks.title: fix(component): wire validators and fix disableDeploy gates in DsfStrategy

leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer  Producer note: reworked per rework note — added the missing route-detection table test ((path, expected-active) pairs across all nav entries, discriminating against the old bare-literal comparison) and the desktop no-toggleMenu assertion (per-link render asserting the mobile overlay stays closed when each desktop link is clicked). Branch merged up to current `main`. No component changes; tests only.  ## QA evidence  **Tests added**  - `src/lib/components/NavButtons.activeRoute.test.ts` — new table test `NavButtons active-route detection > marks the right entry active for <path>`: 18 (path, expected-active) rows across App/Docs/Rewards/Trade/Chart, covering trailingSlash=always pathnames (`/lock/`, `/unlock/`, `/docs/`, `/docs/tokenomics/`, `/rewards/`), base-prefixed pathnames (base mocked to `/cyclo`), no-active routes, and prefix look-alikes (`/lockdown/`, `/docsy/`). - `src/lib/components/NavButtons.test.ts` — new `it.each` over all five desktop links: `should not open the mobile menu when the desktop <id> link is clicked`, asserting the mobile overlay stays closed after clicking each desktop link.  **Mutation proof (fails on the old behavior)**  Mutation 1 — reverted the `class:underline` bindings to the old bare-literal comparisons (`$page.url.pathname === "/lock" || ... === "/unlock"`, `.startsWith("/docs")`, `=== "/rewards"`): table test kills it, 9/18 rows fail:  ``` Tests  9 failed | 9 passed (18) FAIL ... marks the right entry active for /lock/   → expect(element).toHaveClass("underline")   Expected the element to have class:     underline   Received:     text-lg text-white sm:ml-4 sm:block sm:text-xl FAIL ... marks the right entry active for /docsy/   → expect(element).not.toHaveClass("underline")   Expected the element not to have class:     underline   Received:     text-lg text-white sm:ml-4 sm:block sm:text-xl underline ```  (also failed: `/unlock/`, `/rewards/`, `/cyclo/lock/`, `/cyclo/unlock/`, `/cyclo/docs/`, `/cyclo/docs/guide/`, `/cyclo/rewards/` — the `/docsy/` row additionally catches the old `startsWith("/docs")` over-match.)  Mutation 2 — re-added `on:click={toggleMenu}` to the four desktop links (Docs/Rewards/Trade/Chart): no-call test kills it, exactly the four de-wired links fail:  ``` Tests  4 failed | 10 passed (14) FAIL ... should not open the mobile menu when the desktop docs-button link is clicked   → expect(element).not.toBeInTheDocument()   expected document not to contain element, found <a     data-testid="app-button-mobile" href="/lock" ...> instead FAIL ... should not open the mobile menu when the desktop rewards-button link is clicked FAIL ... should not open the mobile menu when the desktop trade-button link is clicked FAIL ... should not open the mobile menu when the desktop chart-button link is clicked ```  **Oracle:** expected active states derive from the nav information architecture (App owns `/lock` + `/unlock`, Docs owns the `/docs` subtree, Rewards owns `/rewards`, Trade/Chart have no active affordance) plus `svelte.config.js` `trailingSlash: "always"` and SvelteKit base-path semantics — independent of the component's comparison code.  **Category check:** rework note asks (a) route-detection table test failing under the old bare-literal comparison, (b) desktop no-toggleMenu-call assertion; covered (a) with the 18-row table (equality entries, the docs startsWith entry, over-match guards), (b) across all five desktop links, not just the named ones.  **Scoped suite:** `npm test -- run src/lib/components/NavButtons` — 32/32 pass (18 table rows + 14 in `NavButtons.test.ts`) with the fix restored.
leaks.title: fix(component): fix NavButtons active-route detection; remove spurious toggleMenu from desktop

leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer Producer note: REWORKED per the rework note — merged main ([merge-update] commit takes main's side, dropping the prose-grep design) and re-implemented the gate on top (commit 1c6fdb9):  - **pr-review-report** (typed input, no prose-grep): `count_unresolved_page`/`unresolved_threads` read `reviewThreads { isResolved }` via GraphQL with pagination; new `OPEN_THREADS` bucket via `apply_threads_gate` — only otherwise-presentable buckets (APPROVED/AIVET/STALE_VET/UNREVIEWED) are gated, unknown thread state on a gated bucket surfaces as FETCH_ERROR (never silently presentable); `--queue` excludes thread-dirty PRs fail-closed (`threads_presentable`), matching the existing mergeable check. - **campaign-prompt**: producer half folded into step 2z FLEET GREENNESS (THREAD RESOLUTION: fix → push `[3e-attempt]` (one per PR ever) → reply → `resolveReviewThread`); step 7 allow-list extended to thread replies/resolution on own PRs only, after the addressing commit. - **review-prompt**: OPEN-THREADS GATE sits beside the QA GATE; prose-grepping "Actionable comments posted" is explicitly forbidden; vetter records no verdict on thread-dirty PRs and reports them as "open-threads (skipped)".  QA evidence (PR body is pre-restructure; producer cannot edit bodies, so it lives here):  ``` ## QA - Discriminating tests: open_threads_tests T1-T9 (count_mixed_resolved_unresolved, count_empty_nodes_is_zero, count_propagates_next_cursor, count_malformed_is_none, gated_set_is_presentable_buckets_only, gate_routes_gated_buckets, gate_ignores_non_gated_buckets, queue_presentability_fail_closed, open_threads_key) — the tested functions do not exist on base, so each fails on base by construction (compile error); full suite 57/57 green on the branch. - Mutations applied: apply_threads_gate 'Some(_) => OpenThreads' → 'Some(_) => b' → killed by gate_routes_gated_buckets; count_unresolved_page '!isResolved' → 'isResolved' → killed by count_mixed_resolved_unresolved (+1); threads_presentable 'threads == Some(0)' → 'unwrap_or(0) == 0' → killed by queue_presentability_fail_closed; threads_gated minus Unreviewed → killed by gated_set_is_presentable_buckets_only. All restored; suite re-run green. - Oracle: GitHub GraphQL reviewThreads schema (isResolved/pageInfo) — fixtures hand-built from the documented response shape, independent of the implementation. - Category check: issue #1 asks CodeRabbit findings to block human review; covered via unresolved-thread state (supersedes prose-grep per rework note) across report buckets, queue filter, producer duty, and vetter gate. Caveat: the report-loop call-site wiring (apply_threads_gate around bucket_of) and the live GraphQL call are covered by the nix build + review, not unit tests — the pure logic is. ```  CI gate (`nix build .#pr-review-report`) passes locally on the pushed tree.
leaks.title: feat(coderabbit-gate): block human review until CodeRabbit findings resolved

Note the assertion observes the default through a feed id with no per-feed and no category fee: live-chain probing at BLOCK_NUMBER showed category 0x01 has a category fee of 0 set, which shadows the default fee for all crypto feeds incl. ETH/USD (fee resolution is per-feed, then category, then default). Verified locally against the live Flare RPC: full suite 61/61 green including all fork tests.
leaks.title: test(lts): exercise setDefaultFee through governance flow

leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer Producer note: reworked per the rework note — the enumeration is now genuinely single-source.  - Removed the hand-synced copies the PR had added: `FORK_VAULT_COUNT`, the per-vault `WT_*` imports, and `testForkVaultEnumeration`'s parallel address + label tables (which also duplicated the existing `allForkVaults()` loop at double fork cost). - `allForkVaults()` is now literally the single source: the `WT_*` constants had no consumer other than the registry itself, so their addresses were inlined into `allForkVaults()`. A vault address can now only exist in the one list that every consuming loop iterates — adding a vault is one edit and it is automatically fork-tested. Count is `.length`, labels come from `vm.toString` in the existing `testAllForkVaults` loop (unchanged). - Added `test/lib/LibFork.t.sol` with `testAllForkVaultsEnumeration`: asserts the registry is non-empty with no zero and no duplicate addresses. This is a real single-source lock without hand-copies, and it runs on every CI pass (no fork needed). - Verified locally: full `forge build` clean, new enumeration test passes. The fork loop itself rides on fork CI as before. - Per the note, #139's remaining legs (hand-pinned expected rates, fork-block pin policy) are left for issue refinement / a design ruling, not this mechanical test PR.  Branch also carries the previously-unpushed merge of origin/main (conflict resolution), now pushed.
leaks.title: test(fork): add FORK_VAULT_COUNT enumeration to lock vault table length

Forge: baseline suite green before the rework (124 passed, 0 failed); full suite green after (123 passed, 0 failed — two literal tests replaced by one source-driven test); `forge fmt --check` clean on the touched file. Pushed 2a1740207f476cf90433aae1a697b8efe0fdb10c.

leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer Producer note: rework NOT executed as written — the note's premise conflicts with the rainix reusable contract. The rainix-sol reusable's test job maps secret RPC_URL_FLARE_FORK → job env FLARE_RPC_URL (rainix .github/workflows/rainix-sol-test.yaml: `FLARE_RPC_URL: ${{ secrets.RPC_URL_FLARE_FORK || vars.RPC_URL_FLARE_FORK }}`), and this repo's `secrets: inherit` already passes the secret through — so the branch's current `vm.envString("FLARE_RPC_URL")` is the name the CI job env actually exposes, and this PR's checks are green with it. Reading `RPC_URL_FLARE_FORK` directly (the note's ask) would revert in CI because no env var of that name exists inside the job. Issue #67's intent (fork tests read the org fork-RPC secret, no public-RPC fallback) is satisfied through the mapping. Awaiting your ruling on env-name convention before any further change here.

leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer  Producer note: reworked per rework note — added divergent-decimals coverage (6-decimal underlying paired with the 18-decimal share) so the decimals/underlyingDecimals split is now proven by tests, not just asserted; also merged current `main` into the branch (one textual conflict in `Lock.test.ts` imports, resolved as the union, plus `main`'s new auto-switch-guard fixture given the now-required `underlyingDecimals` field).  **What changed**  - Merge commit 3dc6ee4: `origin/main` merged in (branch was 2 months stale); `Lock.svelte` auto-merged cleanly with all four underlying-context call sites still on `underlyingDecimals` and both share-output sites on `decimals`. - Commit f4e1edc: new `describe("Lock component with divergent share/underlying decimals")` in `src/lib/components/Lock.test.ts` with a `decimals: 18, underlyingDecimals: 6` token. Every assertion is an exact value that differs between the two fields:   - typed input parses with `underlyingDecimals`: typing `1.5` submits `assets === parseUnits("1.5", 6)` (1_500_000n), not 1.5e18;   - underlying balance readouts (`your-balance`, `underlying-balance`) format a 1_234_567n balance as exactly `1.234567`, not `0.000000000001234567`;   - MAX fill round-trips the exact underlying balance: input fills `1.234567` and submit sends `assets === 1_234_567n`;   - calculated share output (`calculated-cysflr` desktop + mobile) formats a 5e18 quote as exactly `5`, not `5000000000000`.    A note on harness topology (the PR body's earlier blocker): the tests drive the component through the `selectedCyToken` handle imported from `$lib/stores` — under the vitest-setup mock that resolves to the same writable the component subscribes to (the test-file `mockSelectedCyToken` import is a separate module instance and does not propagate). The divergent token is also placed in `allTokens`, otherwise the token `Select`'s `bind:selected` resets the selection to the first production token on render.  **QA evidence**  Each of the six decimals call sites in `Lock.svelte` was mutated to the other field, one at a time; every mutant is killed (run scoped to the new describe: 4 tests, 15 skipped):  1. `assets` parseUnits → `decimals`: `2 failed | 2 passed` — input-parse and MAX tests fail with `Unable to find an element by: [data-testid="disclaimer-modal"]` (the 18-decimal parse reads as insufficient funds, LOCK stays disabled). 2. `your-balance` formatUnits → `decimals`: `1 failed | 3 passed` — `AssertionError: expected '0.000000000001234567' to be '1.234567'`. 3. MAX fill formatUnits → `decimals`: `1 failed | 3 passed` — `AssertionError: expected '0.000000000001234567' to be '1.234567'` (input value). 4. `underlying-balance` label formatUnits → `decimals`: `1 failed | 3 passed` — `AssertionError: expected 'sFLR Balance: 0.000000000001234567' to be 'sFLR Balance: 1.234567'`. 5. desktop share output formatUnits → `underlyingDecimals`: `1 failed | 3 passed` — `AssertionError: expected '5000000000000' to be '5'`. 6. mobile share output formatUnits → `underlyingDecimals`: `1 failed | 3 passed` — `AssertionError: expected '5000000000000' to be '5'`.  All mutants reverted; results on the fixed code:  - Scoped: `npx vitest run src/lib/components/Lock.test.ts` — 19/19 pass. - Full: `npx vitest run` — 53 files, 287/287 pass. - `npm run check` — 0 errors, 0 warnings; `npm run lint-check` — clean; prettier — clean.
leaks.title: fix: separate cyToken share decimals from underlying decimals (A17-5)
